typologies.name: Structuring / Smurfing
typologies.description: Breaking large transactions into smaller ones to avoid reporting thresholds
indicators: Multiple transactions just below $10,000 (CTR threshold)
Same sender, multiple transactions within short time window
Round or near-round amounts repeated

typologies.name: Round-Tripping / Circular Flows
typologies.description: Funds flowing in a circle back to the originator through intermediaries
indicators: A sends to B, B sends to C, C sends back to A
Similar amounts at each hop (minus small fees)
Transactions occur in rapid succession

typologies.name: Rapid Fund Movement
typologies.description: Funds received and immediately transferred out (pass-through)
indicators: Incoming transfer followed by outgoing transfer within minutes
Account balance returns to near-zero after each cycle
No legitimate business purpose apparent

typologies.name: Layering
typologies.description: Complex chains of transactions to obscure fund origin
indicators: Multiple intermediary accounts with no other activity
Progressively smaller amounts at each layer
Different transaction types used (transfer, payment, cash-out)

typologies.name: Velocity Abuse
typologies.description: Abnormally high transaction frequency for account profile
indicators: Transaction count exceeds 3x historical average
Sudden spike in activity after dormant period
High-frequency small transactions

typologies.name: Dormant Account Activation
typologies.description: Sudden high-value activity on previously inactive account
indicators: No transactions for 90+ days, then large transfer
Account ownership or contact details recently changed
First transaction is high-value outgoing transfer

typologies.name: Synthetic Identity Fraud
typologies.description: AI-generated or composite fake identities used to open accounts and conduct fraudulent transactions
indicators: New account with rapid credit-building activity followed by large cash-out (bust-out pattern)
Multiple accounts sharing overlapping PII fragments (partial address, phone, or SSN overlap)
Identity elements have no historical footprint in public records or credit bureaus
Account behavior perfectly matches textbook legitimate patterns (too clean)

typologies.name: Deepfake Authorization Fraud
typologies.description: Use of AI-generated voice or video to impersonate authorized individuals for transaction approval
indicators: Voice-authorized transaction with unusually high amount compared to account history
Authorization channel inconsistent with customer's historical behavior
Multiple high-value voice/video authorizations in short succession
Authorization metadata anomalies (call duration, audio quality, session timing)

typologies.name: AI-Assisted Social Engineering
typologies.description: Transactions resulting from LLM-crafted phishing or social engineering attacks targeting account holders
indicators: Sudden wire transfer to a new beneficiary immediately after account credential changes
Transaction memo or notes contain urgency language typical of social engineering
Victim account shows login from new device/location shortly before transaction
Multiple victims sending to the same beneficiary within a short time window
